goal: Create small review-only Sentinel TCI raster-window previews for the draft Pacuare planning bounds without committing the full COGs or replacing the active source drape.

method.coordinate_transform: Inline WGS84 to UTM Transverse Mercator calculation using WGS84 ellipsoid constants; used only for review-window planning.
method.pixel_model: Sentinel-2 TCI COGs are treated as 10980x10980 rasters at 10 m/pixel using tileinfo tileOrigin coordinates.
method.clip_algorithm: Transform draft WGS84 bounds corners into each tile native UTM zone, clamp against tile bounds, extract the native pixel window with sharp/libvips, and downsample each preview to width 1024 while preserving aspect ratio.
limitations: Uses draft planning bbox corners, not an official Pacuare route polygon, wetted channel, bank polygon, or protected-area-safe publication geometry.
Does not use SCL, cloud, shadow, haze, nodata, water, canopy, wet-rock, waterfall, or mist QA masks.
Does not stitch the two clips, reproject into a final working CRS, or align to guide-reviewed stationing.

outputs.visual_review: Useful eastern/lower-corridor rainforest and river-context preview, but heavy cloud and shadow remain visible over the western side of the bbox.
Useful western/southern planning-bounds preview for terrain and settlement context, but cloud bands still block source-drape or mask promotion.

windowed_read_decision.decision: Retain active Pacuare Unreal preview on the existing NASA/Copernicus proxy drape until exact clipping, SCL/cloud QA, stitching, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval pass.

forbidden_use: production water or vegetation mask generation
promotion_blockers: Replace draft bbox clipping with reviewed route/bank/corridor geometry in the selected Costa Rica working CRS.
Read and apply SCL/QA plus manual cloud, shadow, haze, river, canopy, wet-rock, waterfall, and mist review.
Stitch accepted 17PKM/16PHR windows and compare against guide-reviewed route stationing before source-drape replacement.
Record Sentinel terms, attribution, storage/LFS policy, and redistribution review before any production raster derivative is used.
